headline: Walmart quietly built a $6 billion business off its shoppers
summary: Retail used to be a fairly simple proposition where store owners stocked their shelves with merchandise, competed with rivals over price, and tried to entice customers through the doors. Larger retailers, of course, have always had a pricing advantage because they can make larger orders. A chain ...
